title: process_content called redundantly on every outbox page view and delivery
description: process_content() (regex-based hashtag/mention/URL linkification) is called on every post every time it is rendered: once during delivery fan-out (delivery.rs:194), once per outbox page view (server.rs:455), and once per profile HTML view (server.rs:1072). The processed HTML is never cached — it is recomputed from content_html each time. For the outbox endpoint, this means 20 regex scans per page load. For delivery, the same post is re-processed for every inbox in the batch. The fix is to store processed_html and tags at post-creation time (or lazily cache on first compute). This matters because outbox and profile pages are public unauthenticated endpoints that can be polled frequently by crawlers.
close_reason: process_content is called once per post render. For a broadcast server with 20 posts/page max, this is microseconds. Caching would require invalidation logic that adds complexity for no measurable benefit.
